Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_UseErosion_toolt: Whether to consider current erosion level. The further along erosion is, the more likely the following chances are applied
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_Garage_tooltip: Chance for a garage door to be broken
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_ExteriorDoorBrea: Chance for an exterior door to be broken
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_ExteriorDoorBarr: Chance for an exterior door to be barricaded, note this happens after the check for a door to broken
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_InteriorDoorBrea: Chance for an interior door to be broken
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_WindowBreak_tool: Chance for a window to be broken
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_WindowBarricade_: Chance for a window to be barricaded
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_WindowBarricadeM: Chance for a window to be barricaded with a metal sheet or metal bars
If a window is to be barricaded with metal this is the chance that metal bars will be used instead of a metal sheet
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_ZMax_tooltip: The maximum z level that barricaded world will apply to, lowering this may improve performance
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_ZMin_tooltip: The minimum z level that barricaded world will apply to, inreasing this may improve performance
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_IgnoreClaimed_to: If enabled, player claimed safehouses will not be effected by barricaded world
Sandbox_BarricadedWorld_OnlyOnce_tooltip: If enabled, barricaded world will only run once on the tiles around you as you explore the world, which should drastically increase performance when going to previously visited areas. This does however mean that progressive detioration/barricading over time will not work on already explored areas. This will also result in extremely large save files as every grid square will be tracked, this probably shouldn't be used and your mileage may vary.
